proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the binary features in the dataset is associated with `objective_response`, with response rate differing significantly between feature=1 and feature=0 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 16 binary features. Strongest positive associations: feature_014 (response 23.5% vs 16.7%, p=5.8e-70), feature_001 (22.1% vs 16.5%, p=1.9e-55), feature_021 (22.0% vs 16.6%, p=6.6e-51). Negative associations: feature_009 (16.6% vs 19.0%, p=3.5e-5), feature_026 (16.8% vs 19.1%, p=3.3e-6). Other binaries non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, several show significantly different distributions between responders and non-responders, with feature_020 lower in responders.
feature_004 (a 3-level ordinal feature) shows a monotone decreasing response rate with increasing levels, consistent with a disease severity / stage marker.

analyses.code:
for c in cont_cols:
    a = df.loc[df['objective_response']==1, c]
    b = df.loc[df['objective_response']==0, c]
    stats.ttest_ind(a, b, equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-tests of continuous features by response. Significant: feature_020 lower in responders (3.44 vs 3.95, p=1.7e-33), feature_024 lower in responders (58.5 vs 60.3, p=2.0e-16), feature_029 higher in responders (3.82 vs 3.80, p=2.7e-7), feature_027 lower in responders (5.69 vs 6.17, p=1.6e-6), feature_022 lower in responders (19.3 vs 20.4, p=1.5e-4). All other continuous features non-significant (p>0.05).

Response rates: feature_004=0 -> 23.1% (n=17592); feature_004=1 -> 17.4% (n=24971); feature_004=2 -> 12.9% (n=7437). Strong monotone decrease (chi-square p<1e-100). Behaves like a disease-severity ordinal where higher level predicts worse response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all 35 features, feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are independent positive predictors, while feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_026, feature_009, feature_022, and feature_027 are independent negative predictors.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression with all 35 features. Largest positive coefficients (log-OR): feature_014 +0.436 (p<1e-15), feature_001 +0.374 (p<1e-15), feature_021 +0.362 (p<1e-15), feature_029 +0.122 (p=1.4e-7). Largest negative: feature_004 -0.362 (p<1e-15), feature_020 -0.037/unit (p<1e-15), feature_026 -0.169 (p=6.1e-7), feature_009 -0.188 (p=3.5e-6), feature_024 -0.0052/unit (p<1e-16), feature_027 -0.0065/unit (p=5.8e-6), feature_022 -0.0018/unit (p=1.3e-4). All other features non-significant. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.032.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three large positive predictors (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) are pairwise statistically independent — i.e. their joint distribution is consistent with independence, suggesting one may be a randomized treatment and the others stratification/biomarker variables.
There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_001 and feature_021 on objective_response (combined effect exceeds additive).
There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_014 and feature_021 on objective_response.
There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_001 and feature_014 on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise chi-square tests: feature_001 vs feature_014 p=0.475; feature_001 vs feature_021 p=0.753; feature_014 vs feature_021 p=0.797. All three are mutually independent — joint frequencies match the product of marginals — consistent with one being a randomized treatment and the others independent baseline characteristics.
Logistic interaction model: feature_001 x feature_021 interaction log-OR = +0.883, p=1.1e-78. Joint feature_001=1 & feature_021=1 (n=8013) response 30.8%, vs ~16-17% in other cells.
feature_014 x feature_021 interaction log-OR = +1.055, p=5.5e-102. Joint feature_014=1 & feature_021=1 (n=6025) response 34.7%.
feature_001 x feature_014 interaction log-OR = +1.060, p=6.8e-103. Joint feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 (n=5986) response 34.9%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong positive 3-way interaction among feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021: joint positivity for all three (and only that combination) confers a markedly elevated response rate, while no individual main effect or 2-way effect is large after accounting for the 3-way interaction.

analyses.code: saturated 3-way logit with main, 2-way, and 3-way interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Saturated 2x2x2 logistic model. Main effects of feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 are near zero or weakly negative once interactions are included; all three 2-way terms are non-significant (|coef|<0.11, p>0.08). The 3-way interaction abc has coef +2.126, z=20.36, p<1e-90. LR test of 3-way term: chi2=425.7, p<1e-94. Joint all-three=1 cell (n=2401) has response 63.3%; all other 7 cells have response 15.7-17.2%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If feature_021 is treated as the candidate treatment, the treatment effect on objective_response is concentrated in the biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1) and is essentially zero outside that subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 (n=5986): feature_021=1 -> response 63.3% (n=2401), feature_021=0 -> response 16.0% (n=3585); absolute treatment effect +47.3 percentage points. Outside this subgroup (n=44014): feature_021=1 -> 16.7%, feature_021=0 -> 16.8%; effectively no treatment effect. Interaction p<1e-90.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 is symmetrically treatable as the 'treatment' label for the same data — i.e. assigning feature_001 or feature_014 as the treatment yields the same treatment-by-biomarker pattern, so the data alone do not distinguish which of {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021} is the treatment.

analyses.code: for trt in [feature_001, feature_014, feature_021]: response in (other two=1) by trt
analyses.result_summary: If feature_001 is the treatment: in (feature_014=1 & feature_021=1, n=4191), response is 63.3% on treatment vs 17.0% off (delta +46.3pp). If feature_014 is the treatment: in (feature_001=1 & feature_021=1, n=8013), response 63.3% on vs 17.6% off (delta +45.7pp). If feature_021 is the treatment: 63.3% on vs 16.0% off in the corresponding biomarker-positive cell (delta +47.3pp). Pattern is fully symmetric. Without treatment-arm metadata, these three features are mathematically interchangeable as the 'treatment' label.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_021=1), feature_026=1 suppresses the boost — patients with feature_026=1 in this subgroup have response rate similar to the wider cohort baseline.
Within the same triple-positive subgroup, feature_009=1 also suppresses the boost.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive (n=2401): feature_026=0 -> 71.7% response (n=2025); feature_026=1 -> 18.4% response (n=376). Absolute drop of 53.3 percentage points associated with feature_026=1. Chi-square p<1e-90.

Within triple-positive (n=2401): feature_009=0 -> 69.1% response (n=2125); feature_009=1 -> 18.8% response (n=276). Absolute drop of 50.2 percentage points associated with feature_009=1. Chi-square p<1e-60.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The fully-refined responder subgroup is feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; in this subgroup response rate is ~78%, while every other patient configuration gives ~16-17%.

analyses.result_summary: Refined responder subgroup: n=1790 (3.6% of cohort), response 78.6%. Complement: n=48210, response 16.5%. Single binary variable encoding subgroup membership: log-OR = +2.92, z=49.6, p<1e-300. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.066 from this single binary alone — roughly twice the pseudo-R^2 of the 35-feature main-effect model (0.032), confirming the responder phenotype is largely captured by this 5-feature conjunction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the refined responder subgroup, feature_004 (presumed disease-severity ordinal) still predicts response — higher feature_004 reduces the in-subgroup response rate, indicating disease severity attenuates but does not abolish the treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within refined responder subgroup: feature_004=0 -> 86.0% (n=617); =1 -> 77.2% (n=902); =2 -> 66.8% (n=271). In a logit with subgroup, feature_004, and their interaction: subgroup main effect +3.13 (p<1e-15), feature_004 main effect -0.384 (p<1e-15), interaction -0.173 (p=0.050) — feature_004 reduces response generally and slightly more inside the subgroup. The subgroup signal remains overwhelmingly dominant at every feature_004 level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the refined responder subgroup, feature_020 (continuous, negative prognostic globally) still predicts lower response, but does NOT modify the size of the responder-subgroup effect (no subgroup x feature_020 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, mean feature_020 was 3.77 in responders vs 4.56 in non-responders (t-test p=8.2e-4); within-subgroup it remains negatively associated with response. In the interaction model, subgroup main effect +2.98 (p<1e-15), feature_020 -0.040/unit (p<1e-15), subgroup x feature_020 -0.011 (p=0.47) — no meaningful effect modification. feature_020 is a general prognostic, not a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No continuous feature meaningfully modifies the within-biomarker-positive treatment effect (i.e. no feature_021 x continuous interaction reaches p<0.05 in the feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_026=0 & feature_009=0 stratum).

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: logit(y ~ feature_021 + c + feature_021*c) on biomarker-positive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Tested all 18 continuous features as effect modifiers of feature_021 within the (feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_026=0 & feature_009=0) stratum (n=4531). No interaction term reached p<0.05. The treatment effect inside the responsive subgroup is essentially flat across continuous covariates — once a patient is in the responder phenotype, the treatment delivers ~63 percentage point response gain regardless of labs/age/etc.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_021 as the treatment, no other binary feature beyond {feature_001, feature_014, feature_009, feature_026} produces a statistically significant treatment x covariate interaction.

analyses.code: for b in binaries: logit(y ~ feature_021 + b + feature_021*b)
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise interaction screen feature_021 x each other binary (full data). Interactions with p<0.01: feature_021 x feature_001 +0.883 (p=1.1e-78), feature_021 x feature_014 +1.055 (p=5.5e-102), feature_021 x feature_009 -0.409 (p=4.7e-7), feature_021 x feature_026 -0.306 (p=6.0e-6). All other binary x feature_021 interactions had p>0.05 (none reached the p<0.01 threshold). Confirms the responder phenotype is fully captured by {feature_001, feature_014, feature_026, feature_009} without further binary modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029, feature_026, feature_009 are general prognostic variables: they shift the overall response rate in the same direction across treatment subgroups, rather than acting solely as treatment effect modifiers.

analyses.code: compare main-effect model coefficients with subgroup-restricted univariate associations
analyses.result_summary: feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027 retain their negative association and feature_029 its positive association inside the responder subgroup (feature_004 levels still go 86%/77%/67%, feature_020 still negatively associated within subgroup p=8e-4). feature_026 and feature_009 are different: their negative effect is disproportionately concentrated within the triple-positive cell (suppressing the treatment boost). So feature_026 and feature_009 act as treatment-resistance markers; the others are general prognostics.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity finding: assigning feature_021 as the treatment, the absolute treatment effect on objective_response is +62 percentage points (78% vs 16%) in patients meeting feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, and approximately 0 outside that subgroup. This is the maximal-effect subgroup definition the data support, including the suppressor variables whose unfavorable values eliminate the benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Inside biomarker-positive subgroup (n=4531): feature_021=1 -> response 78.6% (n=1790); feature_021=0 -> response 15.8% (n=2741); absolute treatment effect = +62.8 percentage points; chi-square p<1e-300. Outside this subgroup (n=45469): feature_021=1 -> 16.4%; feature_021=0 -> 16.7%; treatment effect = -0.3 percentage points (p=0.46). Treatment-by-subgroup interaction is essentially complete: the entire treatment benefit observed in the cohort is attributable to this subgroup. The subgroup definition includes both positive predictive biomarkers (feature_001, feature_014) and negative resistance markers (feature_026 must be 0, feature_009 must be 0). Note: by symmetry the same effect can equivalently be reported with feature_001 or feature_014 as the treatment label; the predictive signature is identical.
